title: DL-024-hyperparameter-tuning
author: AI 面试题库
abstract:
DL-024 ⭐⭐⭐ Hyperparameter Tuning Strategies

 Question
What are hyperparameters? Explain strategies for hyperparameter tuning in deep learning.

 Answer

 What are Hyperparameters?
Hyperparameters are configuration settings that control the training process. Unlike weights, they are set before training and not learned from data.

 Common Hyperparameters

- Learning rate
- Batch size
- Number of layers
- Number of neurons per layer
- Dropout rate
- Optimizer parameters
- Activa...

sections.title: Question
sections.content: What are hyperparameters? Explain strategies for hyperparameter tuning in deep learning.

sections.title: What are Hyperparameters?
sections.content: **Hyperparameters** are configuration settings that control the training process. Unlike weights, they are set before training and not learned from data.

sections.title: Common Hyperparameters
sections.content:
```
- Learning rate
- Batch size
- Number of layers
- Number of neurons per layer
- Dropout rate
- Optimizer parameters
- Activation functions
```

---

sections.title: 1. Manual Search
sections.content:
```
Process: Try values based on intuition and experience
✅ Simple, no overhead
❌ Time-consuming, suboptimal
```

sections.title: 2. Grid Search
sections.content:
```
Process: Try all combinations from predefined grid
✅ Exhaustive, finds best in grid
❌ Computationally expensive
❌ Doesn't scale well
```

sections.title: 3. Random Search
sections.content:
```
Process: Randomly sample from hyperparameter space
✅ More efficient than grid search
✅ Can find good values with fewer trials
❌ Still random, may miss optimal
```

sections.title: 4. Bayesian Optimization
sections.content:
```
Process: Build probabilistic model, select promising values
✅ Sample-efficient
✅ Finds good values quickly
❌ More complex implementation
```

sections.title: Tools
sections.content:
```
- Optuna
- Hyperopt
- Ray Tune
- Keras Tuner
```

sections.title: 5. Automated Hyperparameter Tuning
sections.content:
```
- AutoML platforms
- Neural Architecture Search (NAS)
- Population-based training
```

---

sections.title: Learning Rate
sections.content:
```
Range: 0.00001 to 0.1
Strategy: Log-uniform sampling
Tip: Use learning rate finder
```

sections.title: Batch Size
sections.content:
```
Common values: 32, 64, 128, 256
Considerations: GPU memory, gradient stability
Tip: Powers of 2 for GPU efficiency
```

sections.title: Number of Layers
sections.content:
```
Start small, increase if underfitting
Typical: 2-10 layers for most tasks
Very deep: 50+ layers (ResNet)
```

sections.title: Dropout Rate
sections.content:
```
Range: 0.1 to 0.5
FC layers: 0.5
Conv layers: 0.2-0.3
```

---

sections.title: Best Practices
sections.content:
```
✅ Start with default/recommended values
✅ Tune learning rate first (most important)
✅ Use validation set (not test set)
✅ Early stopping to save time
✅ Document all experiments
```
